perturbation.original_text: Background The Supplier wishes to appoint the Distributor as its non-exclusive distributor for the promotion and sale of the Products within the Territory (both as defined below), and the Distributor wishes to promote and sell the Products within the Territory on the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Background The Supplier wishes to appoint the Representative as its non-exclusive distributor for the promotion and sale of the Products within the Territory (both as defined below), and the Distributor wishes to promote and sell the Products within the Territory on the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Representative' in the background section introduces ambiguity. The contract proceeds to define the rights and responsibilities of the 'Distributor,' but the background now refers to a 'Representative' without further clarification. This creates an in-text contradiction in subsequent sections where the functions of a distributor are explained, when in this particular section it is being called a Representative, which might not be the same. This lack of clarity affects which entity is actually bound by the agreement's terms, as the agreement is for a 'Distributor', not a 'Representative'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Appointment 2.1 The Supplier appoints the Distributor as its non-exclusive distributor to distribute the Products in the Territory on the terms of this agreement. 2.2 Following the expiry of the Initial Term, the Supplier may amend the Territory to remove any Inactive Territories. 2.3 Notwithstanding 2.1 and 2.2, the Supplier may sell or supply the Products to end users within USA at anytime. 2.4 The Distributor shall not: (a) represent itself as an agent of the Supplier for any purpose; or (b) pledge the Supplier's credit; or (c) give any condition or warranty on the Supplier's behalf except for any warranty or guarantee which cannot be excluded under law; or (d) make any representation on the Supplier's behalf; or (e) commit the Supplier to any contracts; or (f) act as the agent or the buying agent, for any person for any goods which are competitive with the Product; or (g) retain any monies of the Supplier except on a trustee or fiduciary basis; or (h) knowingly make any false or misleading statement to any supplier or service provider, or to the Distributor or its business, about the Products or the liability of the Supplier for the cost of any goods or services supplied to the Distributor or its business; or (i) incur any liability or assume any obligation on behalf of the Supplier; or 2.5 The Distributor shall not without the Supplier's prior written consent make any promises or guarantees about the Products beyond those: (a) agreed between the Distributor and the Supplier; and (b) contained in any promotional material supplied by the Supplier.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Distributor' to 'Representative' in the background section creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2, which defines the 'Appointment' of the 'Distributor' and outlines their responsibilities. The contradicted text (Section 2) directly refers to and imposes obligations on the 'Distributor,' while the changed background section introduces the term 'Representative,' creating ambiguity and conflicting implications about which entity is bound by the agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations regarding the functions and obligations applicable to a distributor as outlined in section 2.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Prices and payment 5.1 The prices to be paid by the Distributor to the Supplier for the Products are to be the Price, as set out in Schedule 4. 5.2 Any and all expenses, costs and charges incurred by the Distributor in the performance of its obligations under this agreement will be paid by the Distributor, unless the Supplier has expressly agreed in advance in writing to pay such expenses, costs and charges.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Prices and payment 5.1 The prices to be paid by the Distributor to the Supplier for the Products are to be the Fee, as set out in Schedule 4. 5.2 Any and all expenses, costs and charges incurred by the Distributor in the performance of its obligations under this agreement will be paid by the Distributor, unless the Supplier has expressly agreed in advance in writing to pay such expenses, costs and charges.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Price' to 'Fee' in section 5.1 without updating Schedule 4 introduces a direct conflict. The payment obligations are now tied to an undefined 'Fee,' while Schedule 4 lists 'Prices.' This creates confusion and uncertainty regarding the actual payment amount, since the document is refferring to an undefined term that will affect payment obligations of the Distributor. It will affect the enforcement of how much is to be paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schedule 4 Prices Aria Stem $610 Origin Stem $610 Remedy Stem $610 Logical Cup $450 Logical Liner $210 Metal Head $240 Ceramic Head $340 BiPolar Head $250 PS / CR Femoral Component $550 Insert $340 Tibia Tray $440 Patella $70
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Price' to 'Fee' in section 5.1 creates a contradiction with Schedule 4, which lists 'Prices'. This introduces a direct conflict because the payment obligations are now tied to an undefined 'Fee,' while Schedule 4 lists 'Prices' for specific items. The document refers to an undefined term that will affect payment obligations of the Distributor, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable with regards to payment, as the payment amount becomes uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Product liability and insurance 10.1 Subject to the Distributor fulfilling all the conditions in this clause 10, the Supplier shall indemnify the Distributor, defend and hold harmless against any liability incurred by the Distributor in respect of damage to property, death or personal injury arising from any fault or defect in the design of the Products and any reasonable costs, claims, demands and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, arising out of or in connection with that liability (Relevant Claim), except to the extent the liability arises as a result of the wilful misconduct of the Distributor. The Supplier shall add the distributor to their current insurance certificate.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Product liability and insurance 10.1 Subject to the Client fulfilling all the conditions in this clause 10, the Supplier shall indemnify the Distributor, defend and hold harmless against any liability incurred by the Distributor in respect of damage to property, death or personal injury arising from any fault or defect in the design of the Products and any reasonable costs, claims, demands and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, arising out of or in connection with that liability (Relevant Claim), except to the extent the liability arises as a result of the wilful misconduct of the Distributor. The Supplier shall add the distributor to their current insurance certificate.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Client' in section 10.1 creates confusion regarding which party must fulfill the conditions to trigger the Supplier's indemnity. The rest of the clause still refers to 'Distributor', implying it is the Distributor who needs to act, but the initial condition is placed on a 'Client' who isn't otherwise defined or mentioned in the contract. This contradiction makes it unclear who must meet the requirements for the indemnity to apply, as there is both a Client and a Distributor in the same section, creating confusion on who is actually being reffered. The document is calling the Distributor, Client, however the duties are not being asigned to the Client, but to the Distributor, creating the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Product liability and insurance 10.1 Subject to the Client fulfilling all the conditions in this clause 10, the Supplier shall indemnify the Distributor, defend and hold harmless against any liability incurred by the Distributor in respect of damage to property, death or personal injury arising from any fault or defect in the design of the Products and any reasonable costs, claims, demands and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, arising out of or in connection with that liability (Relevant Claim), except to the extent the liability arises as a result of the wilful misconduct of the Distributor. The Supplier shall add the distributor to their current insurance certificate. 10.2 The Distributor shall, within five days of a matter which may result in a Relevant Claim: (a) give the Supplier written notice of the details of the matter; (b) give the Supplier access to and allow copies to be taken of any materials, records or documents as the Supplier may require to take action under clause 10.2(c); (c) allow the Supplier the exclusive conduct of any proceedings and take any action that the Supplier requires to defend or resist the matter, including using professional advisers nominated by the Supplier; and (d) not admit liability or settle the matter without the Supplier's written consent. 10.3 During the Term, the Supplier shall maintain product liability insurance with a reputable insurer of no less than AU$10 million for any one occurrence for any and all liability (however arising) for a claim that the Products are faulty or defective. The Supplier shall provide a copy of the insurance policy to the Distributor on request. 10.4 The Distributor undertakes to maintain appropriate, up-to-date and accurate records to enable the immediate recall of any Products or batches of Products from the retail or wholesale markets. These records shall include records of deliveries to customers (including batch numbers, delivery date, name and address of customer, telephone number, fax number and e-mail address). 10.5 The Distributor shall, at the Supplier's cost, give any assistance that the Supplier shall reasonably require to recall, as a matter of urgency, Products from the retail or wholesale market.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Distributor' to 'Client' in section 10.1 creates a direct contradiction because the rest of the clause, and subsequent clauses (10.2, 10.4, 10.5), refer to 'Distributor' and assign duties to them. By introducing 'Client' as the entity that must fulfill the initial conditions, while the Distributor remains responsible for the rest of the obligations under clause 10, the contract becomes ambiguous. It is unclear whether the Client or the Distributor needs to satisfy the condition in 10.1 to trigger the supplier's indemnity. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, where the contract uses multiple terms ('Client' and 'Distributor' in this context) without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations and uncertainty.
